mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate for equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack accelerates, signaling an imminent economic slowdown that depresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and decelerating core inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary tightening pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 60% of triggered months, or the interaction term yields negative out-of-sample R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during early-cycle phases, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms economic resilience and supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or payroll growth fails to predict positive returns in backtested samples.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in housing starts exceeds 5%, indicating a severe contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, which transmits to broader equity markets and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 50% of months following a greater than 5% housing starts decline, invalidating the contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a flat or declining federal funds rate signal delayed monetary policy response to inflation, increasing real yield volatility and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative out-of-sample R-squared over 3-year rolling tests, or the signal fails to predict negative returns during high inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product exceeds 2.0%, confirming strong aggregate demand that lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in greater than 65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.0%, or the signal loses significance during stimulus periods.
